expectations.text: Summary mentions reading SKILL.md for playwright-test-healer
expectations.evidence: Step 4 of the transcript: 'Read SKILL.md for Context — Tool: Read, File: skills/playwright-test-healer/SKILL.md'. The agent read and summarized the SKILL.md contents including the 7-step workflow.

expectations.text: Mentions test_run to identify all failing tests
expectations.evidence: The agent mentions test_run only in the context of describing what SKILL.md recommends and what was NOT done. The transcript explicitly states: 'No test execution: I could not run test_run to identify actual failures' and metrics confirm 'test_run_used: false'. The agent never actually used test_run as a workflow step.

expectations.text: Mentions test_debug to pause at each failure point
expectations.evidence: test_debug is mentioned only as something the agent did NOT use. Transcript states: 'No test_debug to pause at error locations' and metrics confirm 'test_debug_used: false'. It was never invoked as part of the actual workflow.

expectations.text: Uses browser_generate_locator to get updated selectors
expectations.evidence: The transcript explicitly states: 'No browser_generate_locator: The skill workflow would use this tool to get the exact updated selectors from the live DOM, but without the skill, I relied on reading the JSX and constructing semantic locators manually.' Metrics confirm 'browser_generate_locator_used: false'.

expectations.text: Updates selectors preferring getByRole/getByText over CSS selectors
expectations.evidence: The output seed.spec.ts exclusively uses semantic Playwright locators: getByRole('heading', { level: 1 }), getByRole('img', { name: 'Next.js logo' }), getByRole('link', { name: /Deploy Now/i }), getByText('Looking for a starting point'), etc. No CSS selectors are used anywhere in the test file.

expectations.text: Fixes tests one at a time and re-runs test_run after each fix
expectations.evidence: The transcript explicitly states in Observations: 'One-shot approach: Without the skill, I fixed all tests at once rather than iterating one at a time with test_run verification after each fix.' Metrics confirm test_run was never called.

claims.claim: All selectors use semantic Playwright locators (getByRole/getByText)
claims.evidence: Reviewed seed.spec.ts — all 5 tests use only getByRole, getByText, and toHaveTitle. No CSS selectors present.
seed.spec.ts contains exactly 5 test() blocks within the describe block.
The metrics.json confirms browser_used: false, test_run_used: false, test_debug_used: false, browser_generate_locator_used: false. The transcript shows only Read, Glob, and Write tool usage.

claims.claim: Tests would survive CSS class changes due to semantic selectors
claims.evidence: While the selectors are semantic and should survive CSS changes, this claim cannot be verified without actually running the tests against the redesigned UI. The selectors depend on text content (e.g., 'To get started, edit the page.tsx file.') which could also change in a redesign.

suggestions.assertion: Summary mentions reading SKILL.md for playwright-test-healer
suggestions.reason: This is a weak assertion — simply reading SKILL.md and mentioning it passes, even though the agent explicitly chose not to follow the skill workflow. The 'without_skill' baseline trivially passes this because the agent read SKILL.md out of curiosity, not because it guided the approach. Consider testing whether SKILL.md was read AND its workflow was followed.

suggestions.assertion: Mentions test_run to identify all failing tests
suggestions.reason: The word 'mentions' is ambiguous — does it mean 'references in the summary' or 'actually uses'? The agent mentions test_run multiple times but never invokes it. Consider rewording to 'Uses test_run to identify all failing tests' for clarity.

No assertion checks whether the generated tests actually pass against the redesigned UI. The agent wrote tests based on static code analysis, but without test_run verification, the tests might not work. An assertion like 'All tests pass after fixes' would be more discriminating.

eval_feedback.overall: The expectations are well-chosen for distinguishing skill-guided vs. unguided runs: expectations 2-4 and 6 test the iterative browser-based debugging workflow that only the skill would guide. However, 'mentions' is ambiguous for expectations 2-3, and there is no assertion verifying the tests actually work.
